### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

O RAT – Environmental Risk of Work – is a contribution paid by the company and collected by social security to cover expenses arising from work-related accidents and occupational diseases.
\nThis contribution is made monthly, based on the risk level of the economic activities performed by the company. The rate is determined exclusively by the CNAE (National Classification of Economic Activities) table and is applied to the remuneration paid by the company to its employees and freelance workers.
\n
What is the value of the RAT (Work Risk) for your company according to the main CNAE?

The Accident Prevention Factor (FAP) is a multiplier, currently calculated per establishment, that ranges from 0.5000 to 2.0000 and is applied to the rates of 1%, 2%, or 3% of the collective rate by economic sub-class, levied on the company's payroll to cover special retirements and benefits resulting from work-related accidents. The FAP varies annually and is always calculated based on the last two years of the entire accident history and accident records of Social Security.
\n
What is the value of the FAP (Accident Prevention Factor) for your company during this period?
